observation_actions.note: LookAt can be directed at actors, objects, or locations. Wave is typically a gesture. These actions can be performed standalone. LookAt can be performed while sitting/standing.

exercise_actions.note: TaiChi is performed standing in open space (no equipment). Punch requires PunchingBag object. These don't require equipment mounting (GetOn/GetOff).

sitting.description: Sitting at location with POI-specific middle actions
sitting.note: While sitting: LookAt is ALWAYS available. Other actions (eat, laptop usage, etc.) are ONLY available if defined in that specific POI's allActions. The actual available actions are indicated in individual POI definitions, not as a generic list.

ordering_rules.description: All actors and objects must have Exists events before being used

ordering_rules.rule: Move before action in new location
ordering_rules.description: Actor must Move to location before performing actions there
Must TakeOut spawnable object before using it
Must SitDown before actions that require sitting (OpenLaptop, Eat at table, etc)
Must StandUp before moving from seated actions
Must GetOn equipment (treadmill, gym bike, bench press) before performing exercise actions
Must GetOff gym equipment before actor can move to different location
Must GetOff if actor needs to Move after sleeping
Must PickUp dumbbells before DumbbellsWorkOut action
Must PutDown dumbbells before actor can move to different location

ordering_rules.rule: TurnOff before moving from music player
ordering_rules.description: Must TurnOff music player/turntable before actor can move to different location

gym_equipment.note: GetOn required before using equipment. GetOff ALWAYS required before moving away. Actor cannot move while on equipment without GetOff.

interactions.requirement: Both actors must be in same location
interactions.description: Multi-actor interactions requiring both actors in same location
interactions.note: Give requires INV-Give for receiver, Handshake/Hug/Kiss requires both actors to perform action. The starts_with constraint ensures actors perform interactions simultaneously.
interactions.temporal_requirement: CRITICAL: ALL interactions MUST have the same 'starts_with' temporal relation between the actors involved to ensure proper synchronization. Each interaction has a different starts_with relation.

laptop_usage.note: OpenLaptop, TypeOnKeyboard, and CloseLaptop are optional (actor can sit down and stand up without doing anything). Also includes optional actions: LayOnElbow, PunchDesk, LookAtWatch.

object_interaction.note: Picked-up objects can also be: Given to others, Received, Used (Drink/Eat), Given back. These form flexible interaction patterns beyond simple linear sequences.
object_interaction.description: Object interaction patterns - not strictly linear

movement.note: Move action has two locations in Entities array: [source, target]
movement.pattern: Must use Move before acting in different location. Otherwise, a move action is inserted automatically by the system.

music_player.note: TurnOn music player/turntable, then Dance. TurnOff is the closing action. Before moving to a different location, actor MUST TurnOff music player. Works with TurnTable, TapePlayer objects.

dumbbells_usage.note: Dumbbells use pickup pattern, not equipment mounting. PickUp from floor, perform workout, PutDown to floor.

bed_usage.note: GetOff IS ALWAYS required if actor needs to Move after lying/sleeping. Actors CAN be left in sleeping state at story end, BUT context switches while sleeping/sitting cause bugs on context switch back. BEST PRACTICE: Don't leave actors sitting/sleeping during context switches. WORST CASE: Re-sit/re-sleep off camera (include actions in graph but don't record them).

regions: right part of the gym room
left part of the gym room

behavior.without_camera_section: When no camera section is present in the graph (legacy mode), the system automatically records all frames from the beginning to the end of the story.
behavior.with_camera_section: When a camera section is present in the graph, the system starts in non-recording mode. No frames are recorded for any events before the first 'record' command. Use 'stop' to pause recording - no frames will be captured between 'stop' and the next 'record' command.

camera_actions.focus_management: Camera automatically handles focus switching between actors, episode transitions, and fade effects. Focus duration: 2 seconds normal, 5 seconds for context changes (cross-episode transitions).
